---
title: "Scientometrics in R"
subtitle: "Bibliometrics, Network Science, and Research Evaluation"
author: "R. Heller"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: [references.bib, packages.bib]
biblio-style: apalike
csl: style/chicago-author-date.csl
link-citations: yes
github-repo: r-heller/scientometrics-in-r
url: https://r-heller.github.io/scientometrics-in-r/
description: "A hands-on guide to scientometrics in R: data acquisition, bibliometric indicators, network analysis, text mining, and reproducible reporting."
cover-image: "images/cover.png"
---

# Preface {.unnumbered}

![](images/cover.png){width=55%}

Welcome to **Scientometrics in R** — a hands-on guide to bibliometrics, network science, and research evaluation using the R programming language.

## Who this book is for

This book is written for researchers, librarians, research administrators, and data scientists who want to analyze scholarly communication quantitatively. You should have a working knowledge of R (tidyverse level) and basic statistics. No prior experience with bibliometrics is assumed.

## What you will learn

By the end of this book you will be able to:

- Acquire bibliographic data from OpenAlex, Crossref, PubMed, and other sources directly from R
- Compute and interpret standard bibliometric indicators (h-index, MNCS, journal metrics) responsibly
- Build and analyze co-authorship, co-citation, and keyword co-occurrence networks
- Apply text mining and topic modeling to scientific corpora
- Create reproducible bibliometric pipelines and interactive dashboards
- Critically evaluate the limitations and ethical dimensions of quantitative research evaluation

## How this book is organized

The book is structured in seven parts plus case studies, followed by appendices.

Start with the *Find Your Method* decision tree (Chapter \@ref(find-your-method)) if you have a specific research question and want to jump straight to the relevant chapter.

## Reproducibility

All examples run against [OpenAlex](https://openalex.org/), a free and open bibliographic database. No paid subscriptions are required to reproduce any analysis. Small sample datasets are bundled with the companion R package `scientometricsInR`. The book is built with [bookdown](https://bookdown.org/) and all code chunks execute during the build; a Docker image is provided for fully reproducible builds.

## Sibling volumes

This book is part of a series:

- [Strategy in R](https://r-heller.github.io/strategy-in-r/) — strategic management and competitive analysis
- [Methods in R](https://r-heller.github.io/methods-in-r/) — research methods and data analysis

## How to cite this book

> Heller, R. (2026). *Scientometrics in R: Bibliometrics, Network Science, and Research Evaluation*. <https://r-heller.github.io/scientometrics-in-r/>

## License

The text of this book is licensed under [CC-BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/). All code is licensed under the [MIT License](LICENSE-CODE).
